The Rubus genus comprises a diverse group of bramble and raspberry plants, which are deciduous shrubs with thorny stems and edible fruits. The health benefits of Rubus species, such as Rubus idaeus (wild raspberry), include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus species are teas, infusions, and jams, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
